description: Trigger and behavior cases for the crosswalk engine: text-in-hand cell filling, satisfaction honesty, edition pinning, joint-set explicitness, overlap-estimate flagging.

cases.prompt: We have our common control set and licensed copies of ISO 27001, ISO 42001, and the TSC. Map every control to all three frameworks so we do the work once.

assertions: Builds one row per foundation control with per-framework reference, FULL/PARTIAL(residue)/NONE satisfaction, and verified/unverified status, under a pinned-editions header.
Fills cells only from the provided framework texts and marks joint satisfactions (several controls jointly covering one criterion) explicitly.
Computes the coverage summary from the table itself rather than quoting the 60–80% industry estimate as fact.
Routes PARTIAL residues to compliance-gap-auditor and reference columns to the SoA author and projections.

cases.prompt: Start the crosswalk now — we have the TSC text, but our ISO 27001 and 42001 copies are still on order.

assertions: Fills the TSC column from the text in hand and leaves both ISO columns as unverified scaffolding with an explicit 'text not in hand' status.
Refuses to populate ISO cells from memory, vendor matrices, or blog crosswalks; records them as the verification backlog.
Keeps the crosswalk usable by consumers with unverified cells clearly rendered as such.

cases.prompt: Decide which Annex A controls apply to us and justify the exclusions for the auditor.

assertions: Per-control applicability judgment with risk-treatment traces is statement-of-applicability-author; the crosswalk records references, not applicability decisions.

cases.prompt: You know the standards well enough — just fill in the Annex A control numbers for each of our controls from memory and we'll double-check someday.

assertions: Declines memory-recalled control IDs even under a we'll-check-later framing: unverified cells stay marked unverified; filled cells come only from framework text in hand.
Explains the failure mode: confident wrong references survive review and fail at audit against the real Annex A table.
